title: Image processing pipeline (resize, blurhash, HEIC)
description: Implement async image processing: store original, generate thumbnail (max 400x400 preserving aspect ratio, JPEG quality 85), compute blurhash from a 32x32 downscale, convert HEIC to JPEG. Use 'image' crate with fast_image_resize for resizing. Storage path: media/{first_2_chars_of_id}/{id}.{ext}. Update media row with dimensions, blurhash, file_path on completion.
design: Storage layout: media/{id[0..2]}/{id}.{ext} for originals. Thumbnail: media/{id[0..2]}/{id}_thumb.jpg. Blurhash: image::imageops::thumbnail to 32x32, then blurhash::encode(). HEIC: use image crate with heic feature flag. fast_image_resize for high-quality downsampling. Update media row atomically.
acceptance_criteria: Uploaded JPEG: original stored, thumbnail generated at correct dimensions, blurhash computed; uploaded PNG: same; uploaded GIF: stored as-is (no resize needed for GIFs or just store); HEIC: converted to JPEG then processed; blurhash is non-null after processing; media table row updated with width, height, file_size, blurhash
